commit.message:
Fix E8 JLink MRAM writes: use M55_HE AHB-AP instead of A32 AXI-AP

A32 AXI-AP (AP[2]) writes go through the Cortex-A32 data cache. After
first boot, subsequent JLink loadbin writes silently fail — data hits
cache but never reaches MRAM. NSRST invalidates cache without writeback,
leaving old MRAM content. M55_HE AHB-AP (AP[0]) bypasses A32 cache.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: mempool protection via P2WSH 2-of-2 multisig locking

Ports the TypeScript mempool-protection pattern to Rust. Locks inscription
UTXOs into a P2WSH 2-of-2 multisig (seller + marketplace) at listing time,
then broadcasts the locking tx and sale tx together as a package at purchase
time, eliminating mempool sniping and frontrunning attacks.

Changes:
- src/services/marketplace_keypair.rs: new service to load MARKETPLACE_SECRET_KEY
  from env, expose pubkey_hex() and ECDSA sign method
- src/services/psbt.rs: add build_locking_psbt (BIP-67 sorted P2WSH 2-of-2),
  build_protected_sale_psbt (SINGLE|ACP on multisig input), apply_marketplace_signature
  (SIGHASH_ALL co-sig), finalize_multisig_and_extract (P2WSH witness construction),
  finalize_locking_psbt helper
- src/services/bitcoin_rpc.rs: add submit_package() wrapper for submitpackage RPC
- src/services/mempool_watcher.rs: track locking_tx_id for package-broadcast sales
- src/routes/listings.rs: add seller_pubkey to CreateListingRequest, return unsigned
  locking PSBT, add POST /:id/submit-locking endpoint, cancel nulls locking tx
- src/routes/orders.rs: buy checks protection_status, returns protected sale PSBT for
  active listings; confirm_order applies marketplace co-sig and submitpackage broadcasts
- src/models/listing.rs: add seller_pubkey, multisig_address, multisig_script,
  locking_raw_tx, protection_status fields
- src/models/sale.rs: add locking_tx_id field
- src/db/listings.rs: add update_locking_tx, clear_locking_tx helpers; updated INSERT
- src/db/sales.rs: updated INSERT to include locking_tx_id
- src/errors.rs: add Conflict (409) variant
- src/main.rs: wire Arc<MarketplaceKeypair> into AppState
- migrations/20260310000001_mempool_protection_listings.sql: new migration
- .env.example: add MARKETPLACE_SECRET_KEY
- src/services/psbt_tests.rs: 8 new unit tests for locking PSBT builder

commit.message:
fix(ml): compute real num_indicators_agreeing and bullish/bearish weights

Two more always-constant ML features fixed:

1. num_indicators_agreeing was hardcoded to 3 → constant, zero correlation.
   Now counts actual indicator agreement: EMA crossover, MACD histogram,
   Heikin Ashi trend, RSI in 50-70 range. Returns 2-4 (how many agree
   on the dominant direction). Varies each tick → real signal.

2. bullish_weight / bearish_weight only checked MACD → binary 0/1.
   Now aggregates EMA(1.0) + MACD(0.5) + HA(0.5) + RSI(0.3) weighted
   sum, normalized to [0,1]. Captures nuanced partial alignment.

Combined with polymarket_price patch (25942ed), volatility_history fix
(57902cd), and purge_old_samples — 4 previously-constant features now
carry genuine signal per tick.

commit.message:
Add BTC relative strength context for all crypto strategies

Extracts BTC 24h change from CoinGecko data and calculates relative
strength for every crypto result (Coin Change% - BTC Change%).

New fields in crypto results:
- BTC_Chg%: Bitcoin's 24h change
- RelStrength: Coin vs BTC differential
- BTC_Label: Quick signal (Outperformer/BTC-korreliert/Underperformer)

Detail view shows BTC context panel with interpretation:
- "Nur BTC-Korrelation" when coin ≈ BTC (±2%) → likely not own breakout
- "Starke relative Stärke" when outperforming BTC by >5% → real signal
- "Relative Schwäche" when underperforming BTC by >5% → warning
- Neutral for moderate differences

BTC data comes from the same CoinGecko API call (no extra request).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(ml): purge old samples before training + fix zero-variance features

Three ML quality improvements:

1. Dataset.purge_old_samples(): remove observations older than
   walk_forward_train_days (30d) before each training run. Prevents
   the model from fitting stale market conditions (old price regimes,
   different volatility environments).

2. volatility_history never received push() calls — was always empty
   → volatility_percentile was constant 0.5 (zero correlation with target).
   Same bug in spread_history → spread_percentile always 0.5.
   Fixed by populating both histories on each feature computation tick.

3. min_samples_for_training 20→50 — 20 samples with 58 features gives
   only 0.3 samples/feature (extreme overfitting). 50 is a safer floor.

Combined with the polymarket_price fix (commit 25942ed), these changes
ensure the top 3 previously-constant features now carry real signal.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: add completion announcements and responsive groups layout (#218)

* fix: add Firestore listener for completion announcements and make groups list responsive

The bot was not posting spin results to Discord because the channel status
listener was never ported from the Python version. Add
listenForChannelStatusChanges to FirebaseService and wire it up in main.ts
with status transition tracking to announce completions when announceResults
is enabled.

Also switch the results groups layout from a constrained CSS Grid (max-width
700px) to CSS multi-column layout so cards fill vertical space first and
spill into additional columns as horizontal space allows.

commit.message:
Production hardening: fix timezone math, add validation, rate limit calls, dedup everywhere

Appointment fixes:
- Fix broken localToUTC conversion in bookAppointment (was treating local time as UTC)
- Fix broken timezone offset in rescheduleAppointment (same bug)
- Fix conflict detection: duration was in minutes but added as ms (missed overlaps)
- Fix endTime in reschedule: was adding raw minutes instead of ms
- Add past date validation (reject appointments in the past)
- Add duration validation (min 5 min, max 8 hours)
- Add Saturday/Sunday to weekday parser
- Reject invalid dates instead of silently defaulting to tomorrow

Call rate limiting:
- Increase outbound call dedup window from 30s to 5 minutes
- Add rate limiting at initiateCall() level (the single choke point all paths use)
- All 10+ code paths to initiateCall() now rate-limited per phone number

Notification dedup:
- Add 10-minute lead dedup to promo webhook (had zero protection)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
